summary: The current Meshy player character is a rejected placeholder because it drifted from the selected concept; the title-accessible pose harness now supports selected-bone sliders and animation preview while the next Meshy pass is prepared from a clean A-pose concept image.
observations: The live test level can temporarily validate GLB load, weapon switching, and shoot feedback.
window.__SIGILBREAKER_DEBUG__ now exposes loadedAssetIds, assetLoadErrors, and weapon state.
A dedicated orbit/first-person asset playground remains missing.
npm run qa:headed:player-character loads the rigged Meshy character and selected animation GLBs in visible Chromium.
Headed QA captured rigged-front, idle, gun-hold, dance, and out-of-HP screenshots at 844x390.
The character visually reads as a strong title/cutscene mascot candidate.
A title-accessible character debug page now exposes manual sliders for the rig's spine, shoulder, arm, forearm, hand, neck, and head bones.
The current Meshy character does not match the selected concept closely enough and was not generated from a single clean A-pose reference image.
The current animation files are separate GLBs, which duplicates the full character payload for each animation.
decisions: Use live-level smoke for the three initial CC0 weapon assets only.
Require a dedicated asset playground before larger asset-library imports.
Keep screenshot artifact guidance unchanged for future playground QA.
Accept visual playground pass for source staging only.
Block gameplay integration until optimization and a better blaster-hold animation are available.
Export pose rotations in degrees and radians relative to the imported bind pose.
Use npm run pose:harness:player when a local authoring session should write the pose JSON directly into the repo.
Require the next Meshy pass to generate one approved front-facing A-pose image from the winning concept before Image-to-3D.
Keep separate animation GLBs only for QA preview; final runtime should use one optimized multi-clip character GLB or a lightweight animation-only clip bundle.
caughtIssues: No standalone playground exists for scale grid, material inspection, or isolated model screenshots.
Gun-hold clip is a crouched gesture rather than a usable two-hand blaster pose.
Headed QA records Chromium .glb net::ERR_ABORTED noise as ignored only after successful snapshots prove the asset loaded.
GitHub Pages can copy or download pose JSON but cannot write it into the repo.
The previous character acceptance note was too generous; the asset is a rig/debug placeholder only.
Separate full animation GLBs are too heavy for final runtime loading.
risks: Gameplay camera framing can hide model scale and material issues that an asset playground would reveal.
Keeping duplicate full-character animation GLBs would inflate character payload and memory pressure.

debugAssertions: loadedAssetIds includes all three weapon asset IDs

latestPlayerCharacterQa.rejectionReason: Model drifted from the selected concept and was not made from an approved A-pose image.

replacementCharacterPipeline.requiredPose: front-facing A-pose, arms slightly down and out, palms visible, neutral stance, no readable text, no weapon
replacementCharacterPipeline.meshyRoute: Image-to-3D from approved A-pose reference, then rigging, then animation tasks
replacementCharacterPipeline.runtimeTarget: one optimized player GLB with multiple named animation clips or a lightweight clip bundle without duplicate mesh payloads
